questions.question: What sport or activity are the young people playing in this outdoor setting?
questions.answer_key: Volleyball; they are playing volleyball on a dirt court.
questions.rationale: Core activity is invisible to blind listener; happens throughout high-need windows (8.9–23.8s).

questions.question: Describe the playing surface and immediate surroundings of the volleyball court.
questions.answer_key: Red-brown dirt court with large tree, green fence, and grass field visible beyond.
questions.rationale: Setting context essential for spatial understanding; visible in frames from high-need window t=11.9–17.9s.

questions.question: How many players are visible on the court at the moment shown in these frames?
questions.answer_key: Four players; two on each side of the net.
questions.rationale: Team composition and positioning critical for understanding game dynamics in high-need window t=14.9–20.9s.

questions.question: What color clothing are the players wearing?
questions.answer_key: Blue shirts and dark shorts; one player wears striped red and dark shirt.
questions.rationale: Visual distinction helps blind listener track individual players during action sequences (t=17.9–23.8s).

questions.question: What infrastructure or structures are visible in the background of the court?
questions.answer_key: Metal mesh fence, green storage structure or building, and trees frame the court.
questions.rationale: Environmental context establishes location as organized sports facility; visible in high-need window t=11.9–14.9s.
